rows.description: Medenham pro 5th part. an: ad Mich: 1757.
rows.notes: Amounts were consistent across extractors. Description chosen from gemini-flash, as its punctuation matched the ledger image more accurately after visual verification.
rows.english_description: Share of the Medenham (Medmenham) rent due at Michaelmas 1757 (fifth part).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Share of the Medenham rent due at Michaelmas 1757 (fifth part), amounting to £5 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts were consistent across extractors. Description 'Groncar' selected from gemini-flash based on visual verification, as it more accurately reflects the handwriting than 'Grosvenor'.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt relating to Groncar, amounting to £155 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts were consistent across extractors. Description combined from both extractors after visual verification: 'Emney' from gpt-5-mini and 'Bien:' from gemini-flash, as these matched the script best.
rows.english_description: Rent from Emney Backgate payable for a biennial period.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from Emney Backgate payable for a biennial period, amounting to £18 0s 0d.

rows.description: Wild Arish pro an: & 4te an: ad Mich: 1757.
rows.notes: Amounts were consistent across extractors. Description chosen from gemini-flash after visual verification, particularly 'Arish' and the superscript '4te' (not '4th'), which matched the image.
rows.english_description: Rent from Wild Arish for the annual payment and the fourth year, due at Michaelmas 1757.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from Wild Arish for the annual payment and the fourth year, due at Michaelmas 1757, amounting to £6 5s 0d.

rows.description: — Bond pro Ditto pro Gooch (with Interest)
rows.notes: Amounts were consistent across extractors. Description chosen from gemini-flash, including the leading dash and 'Ditto', which were confirmed by visual verification.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Bond relating to the foregoing (Wild Arish) held for Gooch, including interest, amounting to £14 0s 0d.

rows.description: Excheqr Annuity pro an: & Dem: ad Mich: 1757.
rows.notes: Amounts were consistent across extractors. Description combined from both extractors after visual verification: 'Excheqr' from gpt-5-mini and '& Dem:' from gemini-flash, with the 'Mich:' punctuation from gpt-5-mini matching the image.
rows.english_description: Exchequer annuity paid for the year (and half) due at Michaelmas 1757.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Exchequer annuity paid for the year (and half) due at Michaelmas 1757, amounting to £75 0s 0d.

rows.description: Yardleyton Rent: pro 5 an: Mich: 1730. a Mr Rowney armig.
rows.notes: Amounts were consistent across extractors. Description chosen from gemini-flash, which accurately transcribed 'Yardleyton', 'pro 5 an:', and 'a Mr Rowney armig.' after visual verification.
rows.english_description: Rent from Yardleyton covering five years to Michaelmas 1730 received from Mr Rowney, esquire.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from Yardleyton covering five years to Michaelmas 1730 received from Mr Rowney, esquire, amounting to £50 0s 0d.

rows.description: Catstreet Wells pro Sequan: ab an: 1736.
rows.notes: Amounts were consistent across extractors. Description 'Catstreet Wells' confirmed. The abbreviation was re-transcribed as 'pro Sequan:' after careful visual verification, as neither extractor fully captured it. gpt-5-mini's 'Sequestr' was a closer attempt than gemini-flash's '7 an:'.
rows.english_description: Payment from Catstreet Wells covering a sesquiannual period starting from the year 1736.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Catstreet Wells covering a sesquiannual period starting from the year 1736, amounting to £0 12s 0d.

rows.description: — Greenway pro Bien: & Dem. ab: an: 1734.

rows.english_description: Rent from Greenway for a biennial period and a half, from the year 1734 (arrears).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from Greenway for a biennial period and a half, from the year 1734 (arrears), amounting to £2 10s 0d.
